title: P848 p4217 Unavailable Complement Refinement

theoremObject.statement: The p4217 unavailable complement is exactly the affine inverse image of the selector interval 1140 <= k <= 17783088. Equivalently, it is the deterministic residue family t(s) = 4749600*(s + 937) mod 17783089 for 0 <= s < 17781949.
theoremObject.notGlobalClosure: This refines the complement into one deterministic interval obligation. It does not cover the complement, prove it impossible, or decide Problem 848.

samples.note: This is the first unavailable residue in t-order from the original split packet.

ledgerRefinement.decreaseClaim: This is a refinement, not a numeric decrease: the coarse complement is replaced by a single exact interval theorem obligation with a bijective parameterization.

proof: The p4217 selector coefficient satisfies gcd(445517, 17783089) = 1.
The inverse coefficient is 4749600 because 445517 * 4749600 == 1 mod 17783089.
The endpoint availability theorem gives unavailable exactly when k_4217(t) > 1139.
Thus k ranges over the interval 1140 <= k <= 17783088, containing 17781949 residues.
Because the selector is a permutation modulo 17783089, this parameterization is bijective onto the unavailable complement.
proofBoundary: The p4217 unavailable complement is now exactly parameterized, but not covered. The next proof must find a fallback selector, impossibility mechanism, finite subcover, or further deterministic refinement for this interval family.
